items.prompt: A tangent PQ at a point P of a circle of radius 5 cm meets a line through the centre O at a point Q so that OQ = 12 cm. Length PQ is:
A line intersecting a circle in two points is called a ____ .
items.quote: (ii) A line intersecting a circle in two points is called a ............... .

items.prompt: How many tangents can be drawn to a circle passing through a point lying inside the circle?
items.quote: Case 1 : There is no tangent to a circle passing through a point lying inside the circle.

items.prompt: From a point Q, the length of the tangent to a circle is 24 cm and the distance of Q from the centre is 25 cm. What is the radius of the circle (in cm)?
items.quote: From a point Q, the length of the tangent to a circle is 24 cm and the distance of Q from the centre is 25 cm. The radius of the circle is

items.prompt: If TP and TQ are two tangents to a circle with centre O so that angle POQ = 110 degrees, what is angle PTQ in degrees?
items.quote: if TP and TQ are the two tangents to a circle with centre O so that ∠ POQ = 110°, then ∠ PTQ is equal to

items.prompt: TP and TQ are two tangents to a circle with centre O. If angle POQ = 130 degrees, find angle PTQ (in degrees), using that the angle between the tangents is supplementary to the central angle POQ.
items.quote: if TP and TQ are the two tangents to a circle with centre O so that ∠ POQ = 110°, then ∠ PTQ is equal to

items.prompt: The length of a tangent from a point A at distance 5 cm from the centre of the circle is 4 cm. Find the radius of the circle (in cm).
items.quote: The length of a tangent from a point A at distance 5 cm from the centre of the circle is 4 cm. Find the radius of the circle.

items.prompt: Two concentric circles are of radii 5 cm and 3 cm. Find the length of the chord of the larger circle which touches the smaller circle (in cm).
items.quote: Two concentric circles are of radii 5 cm and 3 cm. Find the length of the chord of the larger circle which touches the smaller circle.

items.prompt: A triangle ABC is drawn to circumscribe a circle such that the segments BD and DC into which side BC is divided by the point of contact D are of lengths 8 cm and 6 cm respectively. Find the length of side BC (in cm).
items.quote: the segments BD and DC into which BC is divided by the point of contact D are of lengths 8 cm and 6 cm respectively

items.prompt: In two concentric circles, a chord AB of length 8 cm of the larger circle touches the smaller circle at P, where OP is perpendicular to AB and bisects it. If the radius OA of the larger circle is 5 cm, find the radius OP of the smaller circle (in cm).
items.quote: OP is the bisector of the chord AB, as the perpendicular from the centre bisects the chord, i.e., AP = BP

items.prompt: Two tangents PQ and PR are drawn from an external point P to a circle with centre O, touching at Q and R. Since the angle between the tangents is supplementary to the angle QOR subtended at the centre, if angle QPR = 35 degrees then angle QOR equals (in degrees)?
items.quote: Prove that the angle between the two tangents drawn from an external point to a circle is supplementary to the angle subtended by the line-segment joining the points of contact at the centre.

items.prompt: Two tangents TP and TQ are drawn to a circle with centre O from external point T. If angle PTQ = 60 degrees, find angle OPQ in degrees (using angle OPQ = (1/2) angle PTQ).
items.quote: ∠ OPQ = ∠ OPT – ∠ TPQ = 90° – (90° – ½ θ) = ½ θ = ½ ∠ PTQ

items.prompt: PQ is a chord of length 8 cm of a circle of radius 5 cm. The tangents at P and Q intersect at a point T. Find the length TP (in cm, as a decimal to two places).
items.quote: This gives TP/PO = RP/RO, i.e., TP/5 = 4/3 or TP = 20/3 cm.

items.prompt: In isosceles triangle TPQ with TP = TQ and apex angle PTQ = θ, the base angle TPQ equals 90 - (1/2)θ. If θ = 40 degrees, find the base angle TPQ in degrees.
In a right triangle with hypotenuse OP = 5 and one leg PR = 4, find the other leg OR.
items.quote: Also, OR = √(OP² – PR²) = √(5² – 4²) cm = 3 cm.

items.prompt: The lengths of tangents drawn from an external point to a circle are ____ .
items.quote: Theorem 10.2 : The lengths of tangents drawn from an external point to a circle are equal.

items.prompt: The tangent at any point of a circle is ____ to the radius through the point of contact.
items.quote: Theorem 10.1 : The tangent at any point of a circle is perpendicular to the radius through the point of contact.

items.prompt: A tangent to a circle intersects it in how many point(s)?
items.quote: (i) A tangent to a circle intersects it in ............... point (s).

items.prompt: How many tangents can be drawn to a circle through a point lying outside the circle?
items.quote: Case 3 : There are exactly two tangents to a circle through a point lying outside the circle.

items.prompt: A quadrilateral ABCD circumscribes a circle. If AB = 6, BC = 7 and CD = 4, find AD (using AB + CD = AD + BC).
items.quote: AB + CD = AD + BC

items.prompt: The tangents drawn at the two ends of a diameter of a circle are ____ .

options: equal in length to the diameter

items.quote: Prove that the tangents drawn at the ends of a diameter of a circle are parallel.

items.prompt: In the proof of Theorem 10.1, for any point Q on the tangent XY other than the point of contact P, the distance OQ is ____ the radius OP.
items.quote: Therefore, OQ is longer than the radius OP of the circle. That is,

items.prompt: In the proof of Theorem 10.2, triangles OQP and ORP are congruent by which criterion?
items.quote: Therefore, Δ OQP ≅ Δ ORP (RHS)

items.prompt: After proving triangles OQP and ORP congruent by RHS, the equality PQ = PR follows by ____ .
items.quote: This gives PQ = PR (CPCT)

items.prompt: If OP is perpendicular to chord AB of a circle with centre O, then OP ____ the chord AB.
items.quote: OP is the bisector of the chord AB, as the perpendicular from the centre bisects the chord

items.prompt: A triangle ABC circumscribes a circle. Side BC touches the circle at D, and another side through B touches it at F. The tangent segment BD has length 8 cm. Using that the two tangent segments from vertex B are equal, find the length BF (in cm).
items.quote: the segments BD and DC into which BC is divided by the point of contact D are of lengths 8 cm and 6 cm respectively

items.prompt: If tangents PA and PB from a point P to a circle with centre O are inclined to each other at an angle of 80 degrees, then angle POA is equal to (in degrees)?
Two tangents PQ and PR are drawn from an external point P to a circle. By Theorem 10.2, which relation between the two tangent lengths must hold?
items.quote: We are required to prove that PQ = PR.
